title: AMSEN Visits
shortDescription: Bridging social isolation through direct, empathetic youth interaction with special needs students at the Al Muntazir center.
storyParagraphs: Children with neurodivergence and special physical or intellectual needs in Dar-es-Salaam often face social isolation due to lingering societal stigma and a lack of inclusive youth-led community interaction. Legions Tz recognized that institutional care needs to be complemented by genuine human connection, social integration, and peer companionship.

The goal was to bridge this gap by bringing active, empathetic youth interaction directly to the students at the Al Muntazir Special Needs (AMSEN) center. Through structured, sensory-friendly itineraries featuring creative arts, crafts, interactive music, and gentle physical games, volunteers created meaningful moments of connection.
Every purchased sensory art supply, game, and learning material was left as a permanent donation to the center's therapy department — ensuring the impact extends far beyond the visit itself.

testimonial.quote: The one-on-one sessions gave our students a sense of companionship they rarely get from outside visitors.
testimonial.name: AMSEN Staff
